validation_events.message: Section 'Backend Web Development Roadmap' extracted 1 topics

merged_topics.name: Backend Web Development
merged_topics.description: A structured learning path for becoming a backend web developer.

merged_topics.name: How the Internet Works
merged_topics.description: The TCP/IP stack, packet routing, DNS lookups, and request journey from browser to server and back.

merged_topics.name: HTTP and HTTPS
merged_topics.description: Methods, status codes, headers, request/response lifecycle, and differences between HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3.

merged_topics.name: Browsers
merged_topics.description: What a browser does with a response, including parsing, rendering, and the request/response cycle.

merged_topics.name: Hosting
merged_topics.description: DNS, domain registration, TLS certificates, and what a hosting provider provides.

merged_topics.name: Syntax and Control Flow
merged_topics.description: Variables, conditionals, loops, functions, scoping rules

merged_topics.name: Data Structures
merged_topics.description: Arrays, lists, sets, maps/dictionaries, stacks, queues, trees

merged_topics.name: Object-Oriented Programming
merged_topics.description: Classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation

merged_topics.name: Functional Programming Concepts
merged_topics.description: Immutability, pure functions, map/filter/reduce, higher-order functions

merged_topics.name: Error Handling
merged_topics.description: Exceptions, error returns, when to catch versus when to propagate

merged_topics.name: Git
merged_topics.description: Git is a version control system that allows for branching, committing, merging, rebasing, and resolving conflicts.

merged_topics.name: Remote Repositories
merged_topics.description: Remote repositories are used for collaboration and code sharing, with platforms like GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket.

merged_topics.name: Workflows
merged_topics.description: Workflows refer to the processes and strategies used for managing code changes, such as trunk-based development and Git Flow.

merged_topics.name: Branches
merged_topics.description: Branches are used in Git to manage different versions of code.

merged_topics.name: Commits
merged_topics.description: Commits are used to record changes in the code.

merged_topics.name: Merges
merged_topics.description: Merges are used to combine changes from different branches.

merged_topics.name: Rebases
merged_topics.description: Rebases are used to reapply commits on top of another branch.

merged_topics.name: Pull Requests
merged_topics.description: Pull requests are used to review and merge code changes.

merged_topics.name: Conflict Resolution
merged_topics.description: Conflict resolution is the process of resolving differences between code changes.

merged_topics.name: GitHub
merged_topics.description: GitHub is a remote repository platform for collaboration and code sharing.

merged_topics.name: GitLab
merged_topics.description: GitLab is a remote repository platform for collaboration and code sharing.

merged_topics.name: Bitbucket
merged_topics.description: Bitbucket is a remote repository platform for collaboration and code sharing.

merged_topics.name: Trunk-Based Development
merged_topics.description: Trunk-based development is a workflow that involves working directly on the main branch.

merged_topics.name: Git Flow
merged_topics.description: Git Flow is a workflow that involves using separate branches for features and releases.

merged_topics.name: Feature Branches
merged_topics.description: Feature branches are used to develop new features in isolation.

merged_topics.name: Relational Databases
merged_topics.description: SQL fundamentals, PostgreSQL, and MySQL

merged_topics.name: NoSQL Databases
merged_topics.description: Key-value stores, document stores, wide-column stores, and graph databases

merged_topics.name: Database Design
merged_topics.description: Normalization, primary keys, foreign keys, indexes, and query plans

merged_topics.name: ORMs
merged_topics.description: Object-relational mappers like SQLAlchemy, Hibernate, and Sequelize

merged_topics.name: Transactions
merged_topics.description: ACID properties, isolation levels, deadlocks, and locking mechanisms

merged_topics.name: REST
merged_topics.description: Resource-oriented design, HTTP verbs, status codes, and content negotiation.

merged_topics.name: GraphQL
merged_topics.description: Schema definition, queries, mutations, and resolvers.

merged_topics.name: gRPC
merged_topics.description: Protocol buffers, RPC semantics, and streaming.

merged_topics.name: API Documentation
merged_topics.description: OpenAPI/Swagger, generating client libraries, and keeping docs in sync with code.

merged_topics.name: Authentication Patterns
merged_topics.description: Session-based, token-based, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, API keys, mutual TLS authentication methods

merged_topics.name: Password Storage
merged_topics.description: Hashing with bcrypt or argon2, salts, and why fast hashes are wrong for passwords

merged_topics.name: Role-based Access Control (RBAC)
merged_topics.description: Authorization model based on user roles

merged_topics.name: Attribute-based Access Control (ABAC)
merged_topics.description: Authorization model based on user attributes

merged_topics.name: Policy Engines
merged_topics.description: Systems that enforce authorization policies

merged_topics.name: In-Memory Caches
merged_topics.description: Using tools like Redis and Memcached for caching, including TTLs and eviction policies.

merged_topics.name: CDN Caching
merged_topics.description: Using edge caching, cache headers, and surrogate keys for caching.

merged_topics.name: Application-Level Caching
merged_topics.description: Using memoization, query result caches, and cache patterns like cache-aside and read-through.

merged_topics.name: Unit Testing
merged_topics.description: Testing individual functions in isolation, using mocking and faking external dependencies.

merged_topics.name: Integration Testing
merged_topics.description: Testing how components work together, including database, cache, and external services.

merged_topics.name: End-to-End Testing
merged_topics.description: Exercising the full system through its public API.

merged_topics.name: Test Doubles
merged_topics.description: Using stubs, mocks, fakes, and spies in testing, and knowing when to use each.

merged_topics.name: Property-Based Testing
merged_topics.description: Generating inputs to find edge cases that example-based tests miss.

merged_topics.name: Docker
merged_topics.description: Docker images, containers, Dockerfiles, layers, multi-stage builds

merged_topics.name: Container Orchestration
merged_topics.description: Kubernetes, ECS, Nomad, pods, deployments, services, ingress

merged_topics.name: CI/CD Pipelines
merged_topics.description: Continuous integration with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, build, test, deploy stages

merged_topics.name: Cloud Providers
merged_topics.description: AWS, GCP, Azure, core compute, storage, and networking primitives

merged_topics.name: Structured Logging
merged_topics.description: Using JSON logs over freeform strings, including log levels and correlation IDs.

merged_topics.name: Metrics
merged_topics.description: Using Prometheus, StatsD, OpenTelemetry for counters, gauges, histograms, and measuring methods.

merged_topics.name: Distributed Tracing
merged_topics.description: Using spans, traces, and propagation across service boundaries.

merged_topics.name: Alerting
merged_topics.description: Using SLOs, error budgets, and determining when to page or ticket.

merged_topics.name: Horizontal vs Vertical Scaling
merged_topics.description: Deciding when to add machines versus making them bigger

merged_topics.name: Load Balancing
merged_topics.description: Techniques like round-robin, least-connections, consistent hashing

merged_topics.name: Message Queues
merged_topics.description: Using Kafka, RabbitMQ, SQS for introducing asynchrony

merged_topics.name: Failure Modes
merged_topics.description: Circuit breakers, retries with backoff, timeouts, bulkheads, graceful degradation

merged_topics.name: Disaster Recovery
merged_topics.description: Backups, restore drills, RTO and RPO targets

merged_topics.name: Common Vulnerabilities
merged_topics.description: SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, SSRF, and the OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

merged_topics.name: Transport Security
merged_topics.description: TLS, certificate management, and HSTS for secure data transfer.

merged_topics.name: Secrets Management
merged_topics.description: Managing secrets using environment variables or secret managers with rotation and least privilege principle.

merged_topics.name: Dependency Security
merged_topics.description: Scanning for known vulnerabilities, supply-chain attacks, and lockfile discipline for secure dependencies.
